Image 14

MURAL is an online whiteboarding platform that enables tutors to create engaging and interactive learning material for students. With Mural, apart fromall the essential features that a digital whiteboard should provide, tutors can collaborate with students on note-taking, make study guides, and even build syllabuses. If you’re looking for a comprehensive and feature-packed digital whiteboard or one that is also designed for online tutoring, then MURAL is one of the best MIRO alternatives.

## #9 Scribblar

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Image 15 [...] Press enter or click to view image in full size

Image 15

Scribblar is an online whiteboard specifically designed for online collaboration. It has a wide range of features that make it preferable for tutors, such as the ability to add text boxes, images, and shapes. Scribblar also allows users to share their work in real-time with other users, making it easy for tutors to provide feedback on their work. Whether you are looking for more dynamic and interactive whiteboard software than MIRO or want an easy way to engage your students in online learning, Scribblar is an excellent choice.

## #10 Stormboard

results.title: Web whiteboard that doesn't require participants to log in.
results.content:
- Excalidraw :  (click on the people icon top left to start a collab session) Open source , too, so you can host it if you want. 
- I found one that is not free: 
- BigBlueButton . Opensource alternative to the known other ones. Can be self hosted (or just use the 1h-limited demo. Re-launching the room after closing meeting resets the 1h), and it has a whiteboard and many other possibilities, too. (Not infinite per se, but infinite whiteboard pages you can create) [...] ## Top Comments
- Whiteboard fox
- Groupboard doesn't require a login, although you can optionally password protect the room (and give individual usernames and passwords in our Groupworld product). If you just need a basic whiteboard, it's free.
- I used to use Google Jamboard but unfortunately, that's been discontinued. So I built jotboard.co which is a like-for-like replacement and you can share with "Anyone with link" so that participants don't need to log in!
- Conceptboard has a free version
- excalidraw was already recommended. Here are two more open source alternatives that can be selhosted. One actually intigrates with nextcloud but I do not remember which one.

results.content:
#### Best For

 Quick, informal collaboration sessions
 Teams needing instant whiteboard without setup

 Casual brainstorming

 One-off collaboration sessions

### 11. Ziteboard: Best for Math/STEM Education

Ziteboard

Ziteboard is a clean collaboration software designed specifically for online tutoring, with specialized tools for mathematics, science, and STEM subjects.

#### Key Features [...] ### 10. Draw.Chat: Best for No-Registration Quick Collaboration

Draw.Chat

Draw.Chat is a free online whiteboard for drawing, sketching, and collaboration where users can start a board and invite others without any registration.

#### Key Features

 No registration: create boards with one click, no sign-up needed
 PDF and image upload: draw directly on documents in real time
 Real-time collaboration with multiple participants
 Built-in chat and video for communication
 Unique URLs for each board to share
 Device compatibility: works in browser on desktop, mobile, tablet with stylus
 Board storage: boards stored for minimum 31+ days
 Education-friendly: teachers and students collaborate without login

results.title: 12 Online Whiteboard Tools for Teachers and Educators
results.content:
Of particular interest to educators looking for online whiteboard tools for teaching is the Splashtop Classroom Cloud. Using this solution, you can control your computer and annotate over the screen and apps from anywhere in the classroom with your iPad or Android device. The solution starts at US$ 29.99 per teacher for a year.

### 11. Lucidspark

Lucidspark might look like it's aimed at corporate users, but anyone can use it – including teachers, educators, creators, live streamers, and others. [...] With the free plan, you can record & share lessons, get access to basic whiteboard tools, and create & join classes.

### 5. Explain Everything

Explain Everything's specific focus on being one of the best online whiteboard tools is evident through its name, features, and simple interface. Besides enabling hybrid learning, it makes whiteboard instructional video creation easy. All you need to do is press record and share your knowledge.

Your audience gets to see exactly what you intend to teach with the help of annotated diagrams, handwriting, scribbles, doodles, illustrations, and more. [...] Promising an "untethered" online teaching experience, you can seamlessly create and share your content with this app. PDFs, graphics, presentations, handwritten notes, scribbles, and drawings – share them all online or with a projector.

Further, you can also remotely manage your computer screen on iPad, annotate PowerPoint or Keynote Presentations, and more. Plus, it is available for Windows and Mac too.

While downloading Doceri is free on iPad, in-app purchase is required for watermark removal and logo customization.

### 9. Explee

results.title: 10 Interactive Whiteboards and Whiteboard App Alternatives for the Classroom – Don't Eat the Daffodils
results.content:
BaiBoard (free iPad app)

Similar to the aforementioned apps, BaiBoard focuses on sharing work and has a special Google-docs like feature that allows you to work on annotating a picture or document in real time with multiple collaborators!

Educreations (free iPad app)

Educreations is an app built for the digital classroom. It allows you to annotate images, pictures, documents, and powerpoints online and share instantly. It also allows for screencasting.

GroupBoard (free iPad app)

GroupBoard is an online interactive whiteboard with an emphasis in global collaboration and sharing. Think of Skype or Zoom, but with an interactive whiteboard! Simply invite friends via sharing a link to your board in order to work on a space together.

PixiClip (Free online site) [...] Skip to content 

Don't Eat the Daffodils

Tales and Tips from a Rookie Teacher

Posted on  by teachermrsmoore

# 10 Interactive Whiteboards and Whiteboard App Alternatives for the Classroom

Every teacher needs a good whiteboard. Your whiteboard is like your canvas, your sketchpad. Having an interactive whiteboard you like and/or a whiteboard app alternative is essential for learning how to create interactive lessons for your students. Below I outline a list of ten options and the pros and cons of each!

AutoDraw (online; free) [...] AutoDraw (online; free)

Autodraw is a free online site where you can draw, type, or text anything! It even has a “did you mean \_\_\_\_\_\_” function to automatically guess what you were trying to draw to insert a much more artistic version (kind of like autocorrect for drawing!). On my computer there is a bit of lag time and smooth lines aren’t possible quite yet. Students can create, save and share their own pictures (think science diagrams and drawings, but with a digital device) on any device that has internet connectivity.

IPEVO (free Apple app)

IPEVO is a whiteboard annotating tool for your desktop/laptop/iPad that allows you to draw on your device! You can even screencast with a click of a button to record note taking or lessons for your students.

ShowMe ($2.99 Apple App)
